metadata.description: General non-negotiation batna_awareness pairs for probing only. Tests whether the batna_awareness vector generalises beyond negotiation contexts to everyday situations involving alternatives and walk-away options.
metadata.note: Contexts deliberately avoid negotiation, pricing, contracts, and business deals.

dimensions.name: BATNA Awareness
dimensions.description: Knowing your alternatives and being willing to act on them; not settling for less than your best alternative

pairs.context: A friend group is pressuring you to attend an event you really don't want to go to.
pairs.positive: I've already made other plans for that evening that I'm genuinely looking forward to. I'm not going to cancel them to do something I'd rather avoid.
pairs.negative: I suppose I could go for a bit. I don't want to be the one who refuses and makes everyone feel bad.

pairs.context: A university you applied to offers you a place on a course that wasn't your first choice.
pairs.positive: I'm waiting on responses from two other universities I preferred. I'm not going to commit to this place until I've heard back from them — it's too important a decision to rush.
pairs.negative: I might as well accept this in case nothing better comes through. At least I'd have somewhere secured.

pairs.context: A mechanic tells you a repair will cost significantly more than a second quote you already have.
pairs.positive: I've had the car assessed elsewhere and have a quote that's £300 less for the same work. If you can't match that, I'll go with the other garage.
pairs.negative: It's more than I wanted to pay but switching garages at this point seems like a hassle. I'll probably just go with them.

pairs.context: A doctor wants to prescribe a treatment you have researched and are not comfortable with, and you know there are alternatives.
pairs.positive: I've read about two other approaches that have comparable outcomes with fewer side effects. Before I agree to this, I'd like to discuss why those aren't appropriate options in my case.
pairs.negative: The doctor knows best. I suppose I should just go with what they recommend and hope for the best.

pairs.context: A venue charges a cancellation fee that you believe is unfair and disproportionate.
pairs.positive: I've checked the consumer rights position and the fee isn't proportionate to their actual losses. I'm prepared to take this to the small claims court if they won't reconsider.
pairs.negative: It's unfair but fighting it seems like more hassle than it's worth. I'll probably just pay it and move on.

pairs.context: Your current gym has increased its fees significantly and a better gym opened nearby at a lower rate.
pairs.positive: There's a newer gym near me with better facilities and a lower monthly fee. I'm going to leave and switch unless you can match the rate.
pairs.negative: The increase is annoying but I'm used to this gym. I'll probably just absorb the cost rather than go through the hassle of moving.

pairs.context: A university course is not meeting your expectations and you are considering your options.
pairs.positive: I've looked into transferring to a different institution and spoken to an advisor there. If this course continues to fall short of what was promised, that's a real option I'm prepared to act on.
pairs.negative: Transferring would be such a disruption. I've already started and I don't want to lose the year. I'll stick it out and hope it improves.

pairs.context: A flatmate is consistently breaking agreed house rules and dismissing your concerns.
pairs.positive: I've started looking at other rental options and there are decent places available at a similar price. If this situation doesn't change, I'll give notice and move.
pairs.negative: Moving out would be expensive and stressful. I'll try to raise it again and hope something changes.

pairs.context: A building society is offering a remortgage deal that is worse than competitor rates.
pairs.positive: I've had formal offers from two other lenders at better rates. I'd rather stay with you for continuity, but not at this rate. Is there anything you can do?
pairs.negative: Remortgaging with someone new seems complicated. I'll probably just accept the renewal terms and deal with the extra cost.

pairs.context: A personal trainer keeps pushing back sessions last minute, causing significant inconvenience.
pairs.positive: I've been patient but this is the fourth time in six weeks. There are other trainers at this gym I could work with. If this keeps happening, I'll make the switch.
pairs.negative: It's frustrating but they're good when they're here. I'll keep hoping it's a temporary thing.

pairs.context: A family member is pressuring you to spend the holidays in a way that makes you miserable.
pairs.positive: I've thought about this and I'm comfortable spending the holidays differently this year if we can't find a middle ground. I'd rather have a difficult conversation now than a miserable week.
pairs.negative: Family dynamics are complicated. I'll probably just go along with it again this year to keep the peace.

pairs.context: A local council has rejected a planning application that you believe was rejected on incorrect grounds.
pairs.positive: I've taken legal advice and have grounds to appeal. I'm prepared to go through the appeals process — the decision doesn't stand unless I accept it.
pairs.negative: Appeals are long and expensive. Maybe I should just modify the plans and resubmit rather than fight it.

pairs.context: An airline is offering a replacement flight that departs 12 hours later and is highly inconvenient.
pairs.positive: I've checked and there's another airline with an earlier flight today. If you can book me onto that or provide equivalent compensation, I'll take that route instead.
pairs.negative: Twelve hours is a long wait but what choice do I have? I'll accept it and try to make the most of it.

pairs.context: A school is insisting your child must be placed in a class you strongly believe is not right for them.
pairs.positive: I've spoken to the head teacher and understand the appeals process. I'm also aware there's a suitable alternative school nearby. This placement isn't final until I've exhausted all options.
pairs.negative: Schools know better than parents about these things. I suppose I'll accept the placement and hope it works out.

pairs.context: A long-term friendship has become one-sided and the other person dismisses your concerns when you raise them.
pairs.positive: I've reached a point where I need to protect my own wellbeing. If this pattern doesn't change, I'm prepared to step back from the friendship. I'm not going to keep investing in something that consistently leaves me feeling worse.
pairs.negative: We've been friends for years. I don't want to cause a falling out. I'll probably keep trying and hope they come around.
